title: Kimi K2.7 Code is generally available in GitHub Copilot
titleZh: Kimi K2.7 代码已在 GitHub Copilot 中公开
author: unliftedq
relevanceReason: 帖子介绍了 Kimi K2.7 Code 开源模型在 GitHub Copilot 中的正式可用，涉及 AI 编程助手、开源模型、开发工具集成等核心科技话题。
summaryRaw: Kimi K2.7 Code, an open-weight model, is now generally available in GitHub Copilot. This is the first open-weight model offered as a selectable option in the Copilot model picker, giving you more choice and a lower-cost option for your coding workflows. Kimi K2.7 Code is hosted by GitHub on Microsoft Azure. This model is billed at provider list pricing under usage-based billing. See GitHub Copilot’s pricing for models and requests for details.

Availability in GitHub Copilot Kimi K2.7 Code is beginning to roll out to Copilot Pro, Pro+, and Max plans. You’ll be able to select the model in the model picker in Visual Studio Code. Rollout will be gradual and we’ll continue to monitor the model’s quality and performance. We’ll expand to Copilot Business, Enterprise, and additional surfaces over the coming weeks. Check back soon if you don’t see it yet. You’ll be able to select the model in the model picker in:

Visual Studio Code version 1.127.0 or later Visual Studio version 17.14.6 or later Copilot CLI GitHub Copilot cloud agent GitHub Copilot App github.com GitHub Mobile iOS and Android JetBrains version 1.9.1-251 or later Xcode Eclipse

Enabling access for Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise Kimi K2.7 Code is off by default for Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise. Plan administrators must enable the Kimi K2.7 Code policy in Copilot settings before anyone in their organization can select it. If the policy is left off, the model stays unavailable to that organization. We recommend administrators review open-weight models against their own security, compliance, and data-governance requirements before enabling them. Learn more To explore all models available in GitHub Copilot, see our documentation on supported models and choosing the right AI model for your task.
Join the discussion in GitHub Community to share your feedback.
summaryZh: Kimi K2.7 Code 开源模型现已在 GitHub Copilot 中正式可用，是首个作为可选选项提供的开源权重模型。
该模型由 GitHub 托管在 Microsoft Azure 上，按使用量计费，为开发者提供更低成本的编程工作流选择。
目前正逐步向 Copilot Pro、Pro+ 和 Max 计划用户推出，后续将扩展至 Business 和 Enterprise 版本。
highlightsZh: 首个在 Copilot 模型选择器中提供的开源权重模型
支持 VS Code、Visual Studio、JetBrains、Xcode 等多个主流开发环境
企业版默认关闭，需管理员手动启用并评估安全合规要求
按提供商列表价格进行基于使用量的计费
GitHub 将持续监控模型质量和性能表现

comments.author: c7b
comments.textRawHtml:
Gotta say, I've lost all interest in cloud-based AI products. Too many cool features and workflows that I was once excited about that I can't or don't use anymore for a variety of reasons (price hikes, subjectively nerfed, disappeared altogether, replaced,...) for me to even remember. It's tiring.

I've set up a small rig, mostly settled on Qwen3.6 and I'm slowly adding features myself. It probably can't compete with Claude. I don't even know, I've stopped checking. It's providing a ton of value to me as is, and it only keeps getting better. All it takes is to realize that it doesn't actually matter if the grass is (maybe even objectively) greener somewhere else. Feels so good to know that it won't change under my feet. I've got this amazing, highly extensible tool, and it's mine.
comments.textZhHtml:
不得不说，我对基于云的人工智能产品失去了所有兴趣。有太多我曾经兴奋过的很酷的功能和工作流程，但由于各种原因（价格上涨、主观削弱、完全消失、被取代……），我无法或不再使用它们，我什至都记不清了。很累。

我已经搭建了一个小型设备，主要是在 Qwen3.6 上，我自己正在慢慢添加功能。恐怕无法与克劳德竞争。我什至不知道，我已经停止检查了。它为我提供了大量的价值，而且只会变得更好。所需要的只是意识到，其他地方的草是否（甚至客观上）更绿实际上并不重要。知道它在我脚下不会改变，感觉真好。我拥有这个令人惊叹的、高度可扩展的工具，它是我的。

children.author: kamranjon
children.textRawHtml:
I'm really happy this is one of the top comments here, I am fully local as well.

Just wanted to leave a note for folks who might not have the memory to run a big 32gb model - I just found out there are some pruned models that have really good performance and If I had a smaller machine I might try this pruned unsloth Q4 quant of GLM 4.7 flash that sits at 14gb: https://huggingface.co/unsloth/GLM-4.7-Flash-REAP-23B-A3B-GG...

I usually use LM Studio for this type of thing but unsloth has their own studio type app that might be even better suited for these quants.

I used GLM 4.7 flash as my main model for months and it was an incredibly tenacious model and very very fast - I think on restricted hardware, this could be a great choice.
children.textZhHtml:
我真的很高兴这是这里的热门评论之一，我也完全是本地人。

只是想给那些可能没有内存运行大型 32GB 型号的人留下一条注释 - 我刚刚发现有一些修剪过的型号具有非常好的性能，如果我有一台较小的机器，我可能会尝试这个修剪过的不懒惰的 Q4 Quant 的 GLM 4.7 闪存，容量为 14GB： https://huggingface.co/unsloth/GLM-4.7-Flash-REAP-23B-A3B-GG...

我通常使用 LM Studio 来处理此类事情，但 Unsloth 有自己的工作室类型应用程序，可能更适合这些量化分析师。

我使用 GLM 4.7 flash 作为我的主要模型几个月了，它是一个非常顽强的模型，速度非常快 - 我认为在受限的硬件上，这可能是一个很棒的选择选择。

children.author: unleaded
children.textRawHtml: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-UD-Q4_K_M runs at about 11 tokens/second on my poor old 1060. Absolutely nuts how far we've come
children.textZhHtml: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-UD-Q4_K_M 在我可怜的旧 1060 上以大约 11 个令牌/秒的速度运行。绝对疯狂我们已经走了多远

children.author: piyh
children.textRawHtml: I tried running any model on my 1070 and it instantly crashes my old tower, probably time to get off windows and run linux on it.
children.textZhHtml: 我尝试在我的 1070 上运行任何型号，它立即使我的旧塔崩溃，可能是时候离开 Windows 并在其上运行 Linux 了。

children.author: greenavocado
children.textRawHtml: Sounds like a hardware issue, though NVIDIA driver issues can't be ruled out, they're much rarer these days
children.textZhHtml: 听起来像是硬件问题，虽然不能排除 NVIDIA 驱动程序问题，但现在这种情况已经很少见了

children.author: SV_BubbleTime
children.textRawHtml:
Understated how much of a boon for Linux that AI development has been.

There isn’t any benefit to running a windows machine.
children.textZhHtml:
低估了人工智能开发给 Linux 带来的好处。

运行 Windows 机器没有任何好处。

children.author: selectodude
children.textRawHtml: Au contraire, I run models on WSL and my desktop reliably wakes up from sleep. Best of both worlds.
children.textZhHtml: 相反，我在 WSL 上运行模型，并且我的桌面能够可靠地从睡眠中唤醒。两全其美。

children.author: broodbucket
children.textRawHtml: Mind sharing your llama.cpp settings for that?
children.textZhHtml: 介意分享您的 llama.cpp 设置吗？

children.author: unleaded
children.textRawHtml:
.\llama-server.exe -m ..\Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-UD-Q4_K_M.gguf -ngl 999 --n-cpu-moe 41 -c 262144 --port 8081 --flash-attn on --cache-type-k turbo4 --cache-type-v turbo3 --no-mmap --mlock --host 0.0.0.0 -t 8 -tb 8 -np 1
Using this llama.cpp fork https://github.com/TheTom/llama-cpp-turboquant and mostly copying from this video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8F_5pdcD3HY

Haven't had much time to test it other than asking a few questions & changing some HTML in cline so it might be thick as a brick for all I know, but still worth trying
children.textZhHtml:
.\llama-server.exe -m ..\Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-UD-Q4_K_M.gguf -ngl 999 --n-cpu-moe 41 -c 262144 --端口 8081 --flash-attn on --cache-type-k Turbo4 --cache-type-v Turbo3 --no-mmap --mlock --host 0.0.0.0 -t 8 -tb 8 -np 1
使用这个 llama.cpp fork https://github.com/TheTom/llama-cpp-turboquant 并主要从该视频复制 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8F_5pdcD3HY

除了问几个问题和更改 cline 中的一些 HTML 之外，没有太多时间来测试它，因此据我所知，它可能像砖一样厚，但仍然值得尝试

children.author: unleaded
children.textRawHtml: I just tested it with some risc-v code and it wrote down a "mov" instruction several times.. yeah something needs tuning maybe
children.textZhHtml: 我刚刚用一些risc-v代码测试了一下，它连续写了好几次“mov”指令……嗯，也许有些地方需要调整一下

children.author: deadbabe
children.textRawHtml:
People want to make it seem like you need to always use the latest and greatest frontier models to be taken seriously as a developer.

You really don’t need them. After a certain point, bigger models give diminishing returns. If you can get 80% of the productivity gain with a free local model, use the local model. It will still be way faster than doing everything by hand, but you also don’t have to pay for tokens to a cloud provider and the tools won’t be ripped away from you on a whim.

This is the new attitude enlightened people should adopt. Reject the arms race.
children.textZhHtml:
人们想让你看起来需要始终使用最新、最好的前沿模型，才能作为开发人员得到认真对待。

你真的不需要它们。在某一点之后，更大的模型会带来收益递减。如果您可以通过免费的本地模型获得 80% 的生产力提升，请使用本地模型。它仍然比手工完成所有事情要快得多，但您也不必向云提供商支付代币费用，并且工具不会随心所欲地从您手中夺走。

这是开明的人们应该采取的新态度。拒绝军备竞赛。

children.author: organsnyder
children.textRawHtml: The biggest appeal of the frontier models is for those trying to get autonomous agentic systems running that do real work with minimal human input. I went down a rabbit hole trying that with frontier models, and after a lot of initial promise it ended up actually slowing me down.
children.textZhHtml: 前沿模型的最大吸引力在于那些试图让自主代理系统运行起来，以最少的人力投入完成实际工作的人。我在前沿模型上尝试了一个兔子洞，在做出了很多最初的承诺之后，它最终实际上减慢了我的速度。

children.author: pimeys
children.textRawHtml:
We've all been through that no? In the beginning you can do a ton of stuff without reading code. But the LLMs miss all the good abstractions, they just push and push unmaintainable code until at some point you start having more bugs and then you NEED that LLM to fix the codebase you don't understand anymore.

There are guardrails you can and must add to protect your team if you take the vibe approach: a good type system, a good database with clearly written business model and a good data model to drive your business. Make it loud and clear when something breaks with your tooling.

But... I'd definitely not vibe everything after a certain point. Reading and fixing code is also a lot of fun.
children.textZhHtml:
我们都经历过这样的事情不是吗？一开始，你可以在不阅读代码的情况下做很多事情。但是法学硕士错过了所有好的抽象，他们只是推送和推送无法维护的代码，直到在某个时候你开始出现更多错误，然后你需要法学硕士来修复你不再理解的代码库。

如果你采用氛围方法，你可以而且必须添加一些护栏来保护你的团队：一个好的类型系统，一个具有清晰编写的业务模型的良好数据库和一个良好的数据模型来驱动你的业务。当你的工具出现问题时，要大声而清晰地表达出来。

但是……我绝对不会在某个点之后对所有事情进行震动。阅读和修复代码也很有趣。

children.author: dmbrThnU
children.textRawHtml:
Made an account to semi-disagree with you, haha!

I have to advocate for the vibe-coded mess-colony.

There are applications where it either works or it doesn't, and it's simultaneously obvious whether it does. Think stock price prediction software. I've killed time in the evenings verbally chatting with agents about that specifically, and what emerged worked! It didn't work well, but it clearly outperformed randomness, and I was able to verify that myself easily.

I didn't look at a line of code, but I had an absolute blast.
children.textZhHtml:
专门注册了个账号来部分反驳你，哈哈！

我得为那些靠“氛围编程”堆砌出的混乱代码阵营说句话。

有些应用要么能用，要么不能用，而且能不能用是一目了然的。想想股价预测软件。我晚上闲着没事，就专门跟AI代理对话聊这个，结果弄出来的东西居然能用！虽然效果不算太好，但明显比随机瞎猜强，而且我自己很容易就验证了这一点。

我一行代码都没看，但玩得超级开心。

children.author: austinthetaco
children.textRawHtml: You couldn't have possibly verified that. Stock prediction based on what? What's your sample size over what period of time? Using what indicators? how far is your lookback?
children.textZhHtml: 你根本不可能验证了这一点。股票预测是基于什么？你的样本量是多少，跨越了多长时间？使用了哪些指标？回溯期有多长？

children.author: ravenstine
children.textRawHtml:
> People want to make it seem like you need to always use the latest and greatest frontier models to be taken seriously as a developer.

Except you kinda do. Try getting a job today without mentioning Claude experience. In another year it'll probably be something else. Saying you like to use Copilot today makes one seem elderly.

Not saying you need frontier models on a technical basis, but for career PR you probably do.
children.textZhHtml:
> 人们想让你看起来像你需要始终使用最新、最好的前沿模型，才能被认真对待作为一名开发人员。

除非你确实这么做了。今天尝试找一份工作，不要提及克劳德的经历。再过一年，它可能会是别的东西。今天说你喜欢使用 Copilot 会让人显得很老。

并不是说你在技术上需要前沿模型，但对于职业公关你可能需要。

children.author: JSR_FDED
children.textRawHtml: This sounds very appealing. What size Mac mini would I need for that?
children.textZhHtml: 这听起来很吸引人。我需要什么尺寸的 Mac mini？

children.author: c7b
children.textRawHtml: Personally, I would always max out the RAM you can fit into your budget. You might get lower bandwidth (= slower generation) than you do on a Mac if you choose a Strix Halo or DGX Spark, but there are always new tweaks being discovered to speed things up. That being said, with 32GB you should be able to fit an ok quant of 35B-A3B or 27B with some context, with 64GB you should be golden.
children.textZhHtml: 就我个人而言，我总是会在您的预算范围内最大限度地使用 RAM。如果您选择 Strix Halo 或 DGX Spark，您可能会获得比在 Mac 上更低的带宽（= 生成速度更慢），但总会发现新的调整来加快速度。话虽这么说，使用 32GB，您应该能够在一定的情况下容纳 35B-A3B 或 27B 的数量，使用 64GB，您应该是黄金。

children.author: sleepybrett
children.textRawHtml: i have issues on a m5/64g with 35b-a3b (mlx) it eventually hits a memory cap around 52gb... but i'm pretty happy with `Qwen3.6-27B-Claude-Opus-Reasoning-Distilled-mlx-8Bit`
children.textZhHtml: 我在使用 35b-a3b (mlx) 的 m5/64g 上遇到问题，它最终达到了 52GB 左右的内存上限...但我对 `Qwen3.6-27B-Claude-Opus-Reasoning-Distilled-mlx-8Bit` 非常满意

children.author: c7b
children.textRawHtml: I'm sure there will be a fix for it, but it illustrates an important broader point I should probably have made above: if you opt for local AI today, expect to run into some issues. You won't escape learning a bit about the tools you're using, the not-so-fun way. I'm not recommending it to non-technical friends (yet).
children.textZhHtml: 我相信肯定会有修复方案，但这说明了一个我上面可能应该提到的更广泛的重要问题：如果你今天选择使用本地AI，就要做好遇到一些问题的心理准备。你无法逃避去了解你所使用的工具，而且过程往往不太愉快。我暂时还不推荐把它介绍给非技术背景的朋友。
